meta.title: Protestant vs. Orthodox: Scripture and Tradition
meta.author: Gavin Ortlund

questions.question: According to Ortlund, what does Sola Scriptura mean?
options: Everything you believe must be explicit in scripture
The Bible is the only authority and tradition has no value
Scripture is the only infallible rule for the church in the post-apostolic era
Scripture and ecumenical councils share equal infallible authority
questions.answer: Scripture is the only infallible rule for the church in the post-apostolic era

questions.question: How does Fr. De Young define Holy Tradition in the Orthodox Church?
options: Propositional content handed down orally through bishops in secret
The life of the Holy Spirit in the church
A set of written documents equal in authority to scripture
The accumulated decisions of all ecumenical councils
questions.answer: The life of the Holy Spirit in the church

questions.question: What distinction does De Young draw between the Orthodox and Roman Catholic understandings of tradition?
options: The Orthodox reject tradition entirely while Roman Catholics accept it
Roman Catholics see tradition as propositional content not in scripture, while the Orthodox see it as the ongoing life of the Holy Spirit in the church
The Orthodox elevate tradition above scripture while Roman Catholics do not
There is no meaningful distinction between the two
questions.answer: Roman Catholics see tradition as propositional content not in scripture, while the Orthodox see it as the ongoing life of the Holy Spirit in the church

questions.question: Which Reformed theologian does Ortlund cite as clarifying that Sola Scriptura concerns the supreme standard of adjudication, not whether the church has any judgment in controversies?
options: John Calvin
Martin Chemnitz
Francis Turretin
Anthony Hoekema
questions.answer: Francis Turretin

questions.question: What Greek term does De Young use for the quality of scripture being 'God-breathed' (2 Timothy 3:16)?
options: Homoousios
Theopneustos
Theotokos
Christotokos
questions.answer: Theopneustos

questions.question: What parallel does Ortlund draw to explain why Sola Scriptura is practically necessary?
options: The early church's debate over the date of Easter
The Pharisees' appeal to oral tradition and Jesus's rebuke in Mark 7
The Council of Nicaea's rejection of Arianism
Paul's letters to the Thessalonians about holding fast to traditions
questions.answer: The Pharisees' appeal to oral tradition and Jesus's rebuke in Mark 7

questions.question: According to Ortlund, what is the key issue with icons — the specific target of his critique?
options: The presence of any religious art in church buildings
The use of images on catacomb tombs and engravings
The veneration of images — praying through the icon as a window to heaven
The artistic quality of religious paintings
questions.answer: The veneration of images — praying through the icon as a window to heaven

questions.question: When De Young says Christ's promise that 'the Holy Spirit will lead you into all truth' uses a plural 'you,' what point is he making?
options: Every individual Christian is guaranteed perfect interpretation
The promise is collective, made to the church as a whole, not to any individual
The Bishop of Rome has special interpretive authority
Only theologians with doctoral degrees can interpret scripture correctly
questions.answer: The promise is collective, made to the church as a whole, not to any individual

questions.question: How many uses of the word 'tradition' in the church fathers did Martin Chemnitz identify?
options: Three
Five
Eight
Twelve
questions.answer: Eight

questions.question: Which second-century controversy does Ortlund cite as an example of both sides appealing to apostolic tradition?
options: The Arian controversy
The Easter date controversy
The Chalcedonian definition
The iconoclast controversy
questions.answer: The Easter date controversy

questions.question: What eccentric view does Ortlund say Irenaeus supported by appealing to apostolic tradition?
options: That the Trinity is modalistic
That Jesus died as a middle-aged man
That icons should be venerated
That women could be ordained
questions.answer: That Jesus died as a middle-aged man

questions.question: According to De Young, why did Sola Scriptura arise specifically in the 16th century?
options: Because the Holy Spirit uniquely guided the Reformers at that time
Because the Reformers faced falsified documents and misattributed patristic works supporting papal claims
Because the church fathers had explicitly taught Sola Scriptura
Because the printing press made Bibles widely available for the first time
questions.answer: Because the Reformers faced falsified documents and misattributed patristic works supporting papal claims

questions.question: What archaeological evidence does De Young cite to challenge Calvin's claim about the absence of icons before the fifth century?
options: Byzantine mosaics from the 3rd century
First-century synagogues in Galilee containing iconography
Roman catacomb paintings from the 2nd century
Egyptian papyri depicting Christ
questions.answer: First-century synagogues in Galilee containing iconography

questions.question: Who is the church father De Young credits with first using the argument that 'what is given to the image passes to the prototype'?
options: Athanasius of Alexandria
Eusebius of Caesarea
St. Basil the Great
Gregory of Nyssa
questions.answer: St. Basil the Great

questions.question: In what context did St. Basil originally use the 'image passes to the prototype' argument?
options: Defending the veneration of icons against iconoclasts
Explaining why Christians are monotheists despite worshipping the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit
Arguing for the authority of ecumenical councils
Defending the use of images in synagogues
questions.answer: Explaining why Christians are monotheists despite worshipping the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit

questions.question: Why does De Young suggest Eusebius of Caesarea's opposition to iconography is theologically motivated rather than purely historical?
options: Eusebius was a Montanist who rejected all material objects in worship
Eusebius was semi-Arian or Arian, so he would reject the trinitarian principle of image-and-prototype
Eusebius had never visited any churches and was unaware of common practices
Eusebius was allied with Empress Irene's political faction
questions.answer: Eusebius was semi-Arian or Arian, so he would reject the trinitarian principle of image-and-prototype

questions.question: What evidence from the Jerusalem Talmud does De Young cite regarding early icon veneration?
options: A rabbi praising Christians for their beautiful art
Rabbis condemning people lining up to kiss images as idolatry — a practice pagans did not follow
A passage comparing Christian icons to the Temple furnishings
A rabbinical ruling permitting the veneration of images
questions.answer: Rabbis condemning people lining up to kiss images as idolatry — a practice pagans did not follow

questions.question: What does Ortlund say about the Assumption of Mary as a belief?
options: It is well attested in independent early sources within decades of the event
It first appears in a gnostic text that is demeaning to Mary, and early lists of bodily assumptions include only Enoch and Elijah
It is explicitly taught in the New Testament but was later suppressed
It was unanimously affirmed by the church fathers before the Council of Chalcedon
questions.answer: It first appears in a gnostic text that is demeaning to Mary, and early lists of bodily assumptions include only Enoch and Elijah

questions.question: How does De Young say the Orthodox determine whether a council's decisions are authoritative?
options: By counting the number of bishops who attended
By whether the emperor endorsed the council
By how the church received and affirmed the council's decisions over time
By whether the council's decisions were immediately accepted by all Christians
questions.answer: By how the church received and affirmed the council's decisions over time

questions.question: What Western council does Ortlund cite as opposing Nicaea II's theology of icons?
options: The Council of Trent
The Council of Frankfurt (794 AD)
The First Council of Constantinople
The Council of Chalcedon
questions.answer: The Council of Frankfurt (794 AD)

questions.question: According to De Young, what problematic theological conclusion did the Council of Frankfurt reach alongside its rejection of Nicaea II?
options: It endorsed Arianism
It justified Eucharistic adoration by arguing the Eucharist is homoousios with Christ
It rejected the Nicene Creed
It denied the deity of Christ
questions.answer: It justified Eucharistic adoration by arguing the Eucharist is homoousios with Christ

questions.question: What does Ortlund mean by the term 'accretion' in the context of church history?
options: A sudden, dramatic heretical break from apostolic teaching
The slow, gradual introduction of innovations into church practice over time
The official dogmatic definition of a previously informal belief
The process by which scripture was canonized
questions.answer: The slow, gradual introduction of innovations into church practice over time

questions.question: Ortlund acknowledges that the proliferation of division is a genuine weakness of Protestantism. How does he argue it differs from a falsification of the Protestant system?
options: He denies that significant division exists among Protestants
He argues that Protestants do not claim infallibility for their interpretations, so differing interpretations do not disprove the system
He claims that all Protestant divisions are minor and unimportant
He says division only exists because of Roman Catholic interference
questions.answer: He argues that Protestants do not claim infallibility for their interpretations, so differing interpretations do not disprove the system

questions.question: De Young compares Ortlund's historical argument against icon veneration to what parallel case?
options: A Roman Catholic argument against Protestantism
A Unitarian's historical argument against the doctrine of the Trinity
A Muslim's argument against the divinity of Christ
A Gnostic's argument against the Old Testament
questions.answer: A Unitarian's historical argument against the doctrine of the Trinity

questions.question: What role does Empress Irene play in Ortlund's account of Nicaea II?
options: She was a theological architect of the council's doctrinal definitions
Her political influence was a major factor in convening the council and its outcome
She opposed the council and tried to prevent it from meeting
She wrote the anathemas that the council issued
questions.answer: Her political influence was a major factor in convening the council and its outcome

questions.question: What does De Young say about the Orthodox view of Mary's bodily assumption?
options: It has been formally dogmatized as essential Orthodox doctrine
It is believed to have happened but has not been dogmatized the way the Roman Catholics have done
It is considered a heretical belief rejected by the Orthodox
It is held as an open question with no consensus
questions.answer: It is believed to have happened but has not been dogmatized the way the Roman Catholics have done

questions.question: What biblical passage does Ortlund cite to argue that scripture has ontological uniqueness as the very speech of God?
options: Matthew 23 (the Pharisees sit on the seat of Moses)
2 Timothy 3:16 (all scripture is God-breathed)
John 3:16 (God so loved the world)
Acts 15 (the Jerusalem Council)
questions.answer: 2 Timothy 3:16 (all scripture is God-breathed)

questions.question: Which Dutch Reformed theological school does De Young reference as moving toward Orthodox Greek theological vocabulary?
options: Princeton Seminary
Westminster Seminary
The Amsterdam school associated with Anthony Hoekema
Trinity Evangelical Divinity School
questions.answer: The Amsterdam school associated with Anthony Hoekema
